etymology.description: semantic 酉(酉) (yǒu, "alcoholic drink vessel") + phonetic 告(告) (gào) -> association with alcoholic drink -> extremity or intensity -> final form 酷
etymology.interpretation: The character 酷 originally combines 酉(酉) (yǒu, "alcoholic drink vessel") to suggest associations of severity or intensity, similar to the intensity one might experience from strong alcohol. 告(告) (gào) provides the phonetic component, contributing to the sound rather than meaning. This combination leads to meanings like 'cruel, harsh' and extends to the modern informal sense of 'cool' as an intensifier in style or demeanor.

examples.chinese: 这个设计看起来很酷(這個設計看起來很酷)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège shèjì kàn qǐlái hěn kù.

examples.chinese: 他是一位很酷的老师(他是一位很酷的老師)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī wèi hěn kù de lǎoshī.
examples.english: He is a very cool teacher.

examples.chinese: 这些音乐听起来很酷(這些音樂聽起來很酷)，让我想跳舞(讓我想跳舞)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèxiē yīnyuè tīng qǐlái hěn kù, ràng wǒ xiǎng tiàowǔ.
examples.english: This music sounds cool, making me want to dance.
